exam.title: MSc Admission Test Preparation
instructions: Answer all fifty questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
There is no negative marking. The qualifying mark is 60 (40%).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the determinant of the matrix [[1, 2], [3, 4]]?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If A is an orthogonal matrix, then its inverse A⁻¹ equals:
questions.explanation: An orthogonal matrix satisfies AᵀA = I, so A⁻¹ = Aᵀ.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What is the derivative of $\ln(x)$ with respect to x?
questions.explanation: The derivative of the natural logarithm ln(x) is 1/x for x > 0.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Solve for x: $2^x = 8$.
questions.explanation: 8 = 2³, so 2ˣ = 2³ gives x = 3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: In how many distinct ways can 4 different books be arranged on a shelf?
questions.explanation: The number of permutations of 4 distinct items is 4! = 24.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: What is the median of the data set 3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2?
questions.explanation: Sorted: 1, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 9. The middle (4th) value is 3.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: Events A and B are independent with P(A) = 0.5 and P(B) = 0.4. What is P(A ∩ B)?
questions.explanation: For independent events, P(A ∩ B) = P(A)·P(B) = 0.5 × 0.4 = 0.2.

questions.subject: Statistics
questions.question: Which statement is true for a perfectly normal (Gaussian) distribution?
questions.explanation: A normal distribution is symmetric, so its mean, median, and mode coincide.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: The complement of a regular language is:
questions.explanation: Regular languages are closed under complementation (swap accepting and non-accepting states of a DFA).

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: How does a DFA differ from an NFA?

options.b: A DFA has exactly one transition per input symbol from each state

questions.explanation: A DFA is deterministic: each state has exactly one transition per input symbol and no ε-moves; DFAs and NFAs recognize the same languages.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: The halting problem is:
questions.explanation: Turing proved no algorithm can decide, for all programs and inputs, whether the program halts; the problem is undecidable.

questions.subject: Theory of Computation (TOC)
questions.question: Context-free grammars generate exactly the class of:
questions.explanation: By definition, context-free grammars generate the context-free languages, which are recognized by pushdown automata.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is printed by the following C code?
questions.explanation: Post-increment assigns the old value (10) to y, then increments x to 11, printing "11 10".

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What is the output of this C code?
questions.explanation: The modulo operator returns the remainder of 7 ÷ 3, which is 1.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What does the following Java code print?
questions.explanation: Java initializes numeric array elements to 0 by default, so arr[0] prints 0.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: In standard C, the value of sizeof(char) is always:
questions.explanation: The C standard defines sizeof(char) to be exactly 1 byte, regardless of platform.

questions.subject: Programming
questions.question: What value does this recursive C function print for f(4)?
questions.explanation: f(4) computes 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 + 0 = 10 (the sum 1..n).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: What is the best-case time complexity of insertion sort on an already-sorted array?
questions.explanation: When the input is sorted, each element needs only one comparison, giving a linear O(n) best case.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Which combination of data structures implements an LRU cache with O(1) get and put?

options.b: A hash map plus a doubly linked list

questions.explanation: The hash map gives O(1) lookup while the doubly linked list lets you move/evict nodes in O(1).

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: A queue is a data structure that follows which ordering discipline?
questions.explanation: A queue is First-In-First-Out: the earliest enqueued element is dequeued first.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: How many edges does a tree with n nodes have?
questions.explanation: A tree is connected and acyclic, which forces exactly n − 1 edges for n nodes.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Binary search can be applied only when the underlying array is:
questions.explanation: Binary search relies on order to discard half the range each step, so the array must be sorted.

questions.subject: Data Structures & Algorithms (DSA)
questions.question: Dijkstra's shortest-path algorithm can give incorrect results when the graph contains:
questions.explanation: Dijkstra's greedy choice assumes non-negative weights; negative edges can break its correctness (use Bellman-Ford instead).

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A pipeline stall caused by one instruction needing the result of a previous, not-yet-completed instruction is a:
questions.explanation: A data hazard arises from data dependencies between instructions still in the pipeline.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: Which addressing mode specifies the operand value directly within the instruction itself?
questions.explanation: In immediate addressing the operand is a constant encoded in the instruction, e.g. MOV R1, #5.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: A defining characteristic of RISC processors is:

options.b: a small set of simple, fixed-length instructions

questions.explanation: RISC designs use a small, regular set of simple fixed-length instructions to enable fast, pipelined execution.

questions.subject: Computer Architecture
questions.question: How many bits are in one nibble?
questions.explanation: A nibble is 4 bits, i.e. half a byte, and maps to a single hexadecimal digit.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: A process in the 'ready' state is:

options.a: waiting for an I/O operation to complete
options.b: waiting to be assigned the CPU

questions.explanation: A ready process has everything it needs except the CPU and is waiting in the ready queue to be scheduled.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which of the following is a non-preemptive scheduling algorithm?
questions.explanation: FCFS runs each process to completion (or until it blocks) without preempting it.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: Which part of the operating system always remains resident in main memory?
questions.explanation: The kernel is the core, always-resident component that manages processes, memory, and devices.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: A semaphore that can take only the values 0 and 1, used to enforce mutual exclusion, is called a:
questions.explanation: A binary semaphore (mutex) toggles between 0 and 1 to guard a critical section for one process at a time.

questions.subject: Operating Systems (OS)
questions.question: In a paging system, a logical (virtual) address is divided into:
questions.explanation: Paging splits the virtual address into a page number (index into the page table) and an offset within the page.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which protocol maps a known IP address to a physical (MAC) address on a local network?
questions.explanation: The Address Resolution Protocol (ARP) finds the MAC address corresponding to a given IPv4 address.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: What is the well-known port number for HTTP?
questions.explanation: HTTP uses TCP port 80 by default (HTTPS uses 443, FTP 21, SMTP 25).

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: Which networking device primarily operates at the data link layer (Layer 2)?
questions.explanation: A switch forwards frames using MAC addresses, placing it at the data link layer; routers work at Layer 3.

questions.subject: Computer Networks
questions.question: UDP is best described as a transport protocol that is:
questions.explanation: UDP sends datagrams without establishing a connection and offers no delivery or ordering guarantees.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: In the ACID properties of transactions, the 'I' stands for:
questions.explanation: Isolation ensures concurrent transactions do not interfere, appearing as if executed serially.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: A foreign key in a relational table is used to:

options.a: uniquely identify each row in its own table
options.b: reference the primary key of another table

questions.explanation: A foreign key references another table's primary key, enforcing referential integrity between the two.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter groups produced by GROUP BY?
questions.explanation: HAVING applies conditions to aggregated groups; WHERE filters individual rows before grouping.

questions.subject: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
questions.question: A relation that is in Boyce-Codd Normal Form (BCNF) is necessarily also in:
questions.explanation: BCNF is stricter than 3NF, so every BCNF relation also satisfies 3NF (and 2NF and 1NF).

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: A distributed system that keeps providing correct service despite component failures is said to be:
questions.explanation: Fault tolerance is the ability to continue operating correctly even when some components fail.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: In consensus protocols such as Raft, a new leader is selected by:

options.a: choosing the node with the highest IP address
options.b: a leader election decided by a majority vote

questions.explanation: Raft elects a leader when a candidate receives votes from a majority (quorum) of the nodes.

questions.subject: Distributed Systems
questions.question: In the MapReduce model, the Map phase is responsible for:

options.b: transforming input records into intermediate key-value pairs

questions.explanation: Map processes input splits in parallel and emits intermediate key-value pairs that Reduce later aggregates.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: The Minimax algorithm is primarily used for:
questions.explanation: Minimax computes optimal moves in zero-sum two-player games by minimizing the opponent's maximum payoff.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Which activation function squashes its input into the range (0, 1)?
questions.explanation: The sigmoid (logistic) function σ(x) = 1/(1+e⁻ˣ) outputs values strictly between 0 and 1.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: Overfitting in machine learning is best described as a model that:

options.a: is too simple to capture the data
options.b: performs well on training data but poorly on unseen data

questions.explanation: An overfit model memorizes noise in the training set, so it generalizes poorly to new examples.

questions.subject: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
questions.question: When all step costs are equal, breadth-first search is guaranteed to find:
questions.explanation: BFS expands nodes level by level, so it reaches the shallowest goal first, which is optimal when step costs are uniform.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: If A is taller than B, and B is taller than C, which conclusion must be true?
questions.explanation: Height is transitive: A > B and B > C imply A > C.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.explanation: The terms are perfect squares 1², 2², …, 5², so the next is 6² = 36.

questions.subject: Analytical Ability
questions.question: Pointing to a girl in a photo, a man says, "She is the daughter of my grandfather's only son." How is the girl related to the man?
questions.explanation: The grandfather's only son is the man's father; his father's daughter is the man's sister.
